title: Blood pressure measuring device and method
abstract: Blood pressure detection apparatuses and methods for detecting a blood pressure of a user are described comprising optical sensors/detectors and a force sensor and, in some embodiments, comprising only a force sensor, measuring force applied by a finger. Blood pressure is measured by applying an increasing (or decreasing) force or pressure with a finger of the user on at least the force sensor, which, in some embodiments, may be a plurality of increasing pressure steps, each step being held within a predetermined acceptable pressure/force tolerance range for a predetermined hold time, and measuring the force applied by the finger and, in some embodiments, optically measuring the blood in a vessel in the finger relating to the applied pressure/force. Feedback (visual, haptic, sound) of the applied pressure is provided to the user.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A blood pressure measurement apparatus, comprising: an optical source/sensor disposed on the apparatus and arranged to receive a first finger of a user the first finger having a blood vessel disposed therein, the optical source/sensor providing an optical source signal incident on the first finger and receives a reflected optical signal from the first finger related to blood volume in the blood vessel, and the optical source/sensor providing an optical reflection signal indicative of the blood volume; a force sensor disposed on the apparatus, arranged to measure applied pressure by the finger on the optical source/sensor, the force sensor providing an applied pressure signal indicative of the applied pressure to the blood vessel; a controller operatively connected to the optical source/sensor and the force sensor and configured to receive the optical reflection signal and the applied pressure signal and configured to determine a blood pressure of the user based on the applied pressure signal and the optical reflection signal using a plurality of optical reflection signals corresponding to a plurality of applied pressures to the first finger of the user; and wherein the apparatus provides feedback to the user of target applied pressures and actual applied pressures needed to calculate the user blood pressure. 2. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the plurality of applied pressures comprises a plurality of pressure levels, the number of levels comprising at least one of: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10. 3. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the plurality of applied pressures comprises the applied pressure being held for at least a predetermined hold time, the hold time comprising one of: 1 second, 2 seconds, 3 seconds, 4 seconds, 5 seconds, 6 seconds, 7 seconds, 8 seconds, 9 seconds, and 10 seconds. 4. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the controller calculates an average optical pulse peak-to-peak height as an average optical height for each pressure level. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the controller calculates the diastolic BP (DBP) by determining the maximum of the average optical heights for all pressure levels as a maximum average optical height. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the controller calculates systolic blood pressure (SBP) by curve fitting the average optical heights starting from the maximum average optical height as the fitted curve; determine an extrapolated optical data point multiplying an extrapolation factor by the maximum average optical height; and determine SBP by inserting the extrapolated optical data point into the fitted curve. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the fitted curve comprises an exponential curve fit. 8. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the extrapolation factor is about 30%. 9. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the controller adjusts DBP by a DBP calibration offset to determine actual DBP and adjusts SBP by an SBP calibration offset to determine actual SBP. 10. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, further comprising a display screen operatively connected to the controller and the controller displays on the screen the force being applied to the optical source/sensor. 11. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the plurality of applied pressure levels each have a corresponding acceptable pressure tolerance to be within to start a hold timer, the hold timer providing the feedback of the hold time to the user. 12. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the feedback comprises at least one of: visual, haptic/touch, and audio. 13. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the apparatus is configured to receive a second finger which applies a second force against the apparatus which is related to the force applied by the first finger against the optical source/sensor. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the second finger comprises a thumb and the first finger is an opposing finger to the thumb on the same hand of the user, and the first finger comprises one of: index finger, middle finger, ring finger, and little/pinky finger. 15. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the apparatus comprises a smart phone, the optical source/sensor comprises a controllable digital camera with a controllable flash and the pressure sensor comprises a touch-screen display. 16. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the apparatus comprises a smart watch, the optical source/sensor comprises a controllable digital camera with a controllable flash light and the pressure sensor comprises a touch-screen display. 17. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the force sensor is configured to detect AC pressure oscillations exerted against the force sensor by the first finger of a user based on heartbeats of the user and the applied pressure signal is also indicative of the AC pressure oscillations. 18. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the controller performs a bandpass filter on the pressure signal to provide an AC pressure oscillation signal at frequencies near a heart rate of the user, and determines the peak-to-peak pressure for the AC pressure oscillations over the applied pressure range. 19. The apparatus of any of foregoing claims, wherein the controller calculates a mean blood pressure (MBP) by determining a maximum peak-to-peak pressure over the applied pressure range, and calculates the DBP by determining an initial minimum peak-to-peak pressure before the MBP occurs and a corresponding applied pressure, and calculating SBP by the equation: SBP = 2xMBP - DBP. 20. A blood pressure measurement apparatus comprising: a frame body having a first side, the frame body including a controller; and a force sensor arranged on the first side, operatively connected to the controller and configured to send pressure data to the controller; wherein the controller is configured to determine a blood pressure of a user based on the pressure data; and wherein the force sensor is configured to detect a pressure applied to the apparatus by a finger of a user. 21. The apparatus according to claim 20, wherein the force sensor is configured to detect pressure oscillations exerted against the force sensor by a finger of a user based on heartbeats of the user. 22. A method of measuring a blood pressure of a user, comprising: providing a blood pressure detection apparatus comprising a frame body having a first side and a second side and a force sensor arranged on the first side, the frame body including a controller; receiving a first pressure at the blood pressure detection apparatus from a first finger and a second finger of a user; receiving a second pressure at the blood pressure detection apparatus from the first finger and the second finger; collecting data from the force sensor during the receiving the first pressure and the receiving the second pressure; and determining a blood pressure reading based on the data collected during the collecting step. 23. The method according to claim 22, wherein the blood pressure detection apparatus further comprises an optical sensor operatively connected to the controller and configured to send optical data to the controller. 24. The method according to claim 23, wherein the blood pressure detection apparatus further comprises a light source operatively connected to the controller. 25. The method according to claim 24, further comprising collecting the optical data from the optical sensor during the applying the first pressure and the applying the second pressure steps. 26. A method of measuring a blood pressure of a user comprising the steps of: providing a blood pressure measurement apparatus comprising a frame body having a force sensor arranged on a first side; receiving a first pressure at the force sensor from a first finger of a user; receiving a second pressure at the force sensor from the first finger; collecting pressure data from the force sensor during the receiving the first pressure and the receiving the second pressure steps; filtering the collected pressure data to obtain pressure oscillation data; and determining the blood pressure based on the pressure oscillation data. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the receiving a first pressure and the receiving the second pressure steps comprises a continuously increasing or decreasing pressure between the first and second pressures. 28. A method of measuring a blood pressure of a user comprising: receiving a continuously increasing or decreasing pressure between the first and second pressures at a force sensor from a first finger of a user; collecting pressure data from the force sensor during the receiving; filtering the collected pressure data to obtain pressure oscillation data; and determining the blood pressure based on the pressure oscillation data. 29. The method of claim 28 further comprising collecting optical data from an optical sensor during the receiving the pressure and determining the blood pressure from the optical data and the pressure oscillation data. 30. A method for measuring a blood pressure of a user, comprising: measuring an applied force to a first finger of the user and providing an applied pressure signal indicative thereof; measuring an optical reflection from blood in a vessel in the first finger of the user in response to the applied force and providing an optical reflection signal indicative thereof; determining the blood pressure of the user based on the optical reflection signal and the applied pressure signal using a plurality of optical reflection signals corresponding to a plurality of applied pressures to the first finger, the applied pressures being within a predetermined pressure tolerance; and providing feedback to the user of the applied pressures and the predetermined pressure tolerance. 31. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the plurality of applied pressures comprises a plurality of pressure levels, the number of levels comprising at least one of: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10. 32. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the plurality of applied pressures comprises the applied pressure being held for at least a predetermined hold time, the hold time comprising one of: 1 second, 2 seconds, 3 seconds, 4 seconds, 5 seconds, 6 seconds, 7 seconds, 8 seconds, 9 seconds, and 10 seconds. 33. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the determining comprises calculating an average optical pulse peak-to-peak height as an average optical height at each pressure level. 34. The method of claim 32, wherein the determining comprises calculating the diastolic BP (DBP) by determining the maximum of the average optical heights for all pressure levels as a maximum average optical height. 35. The method of claim 33, wherein the determining comprises calculating systolic blood pressure (SBP) by curve fitting the average optical heights starting from the maximum average optical height as the fitted curve; multiplying an extrapolation factor by the maximum average optical height to determine an extrapolated optical data point; inserting the extrapolated optical data point into the fitted curve to determine SBP. 36. The method of claim 34, wherein the fitted curve comprises an exponential curve fit. 37. The method of claim 34, wherein the extrapolation factor is about 30%. 38. The method of claim 34, wherein the controller adjusts DBP by a DBP calibration offset to determine actual DBP and adjusts SBP by an SBP calibration offset to determine actual SBP. 39. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, further comprising displaying the applied pressure. 40. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 30, wherein the plurality of applied pressure levels each have a corresponding acceptable pressure tolerance to be within to start a hold timer, the hold timer providing the feedback of the hold time to the user. 41. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the feedback comprises at least one of: visual, haptic/touch, and audio. 42. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the applied force to the first finger comprises a second force applied by a second finger opposing the first finger. 43. The method of claim 41, wherein the second finger comprises a thumb and the first finger comprises an opposing finger to the thumb on the same hand of the user, and the first finger comprises one of: index finger, middle finger, ring finger, and little/pinky finger. 44. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the measuring the optical signal is performed by a smart phone having an optical source/sensor comprising a controllable digital camera with a controllable flash and the measuring the applied force is performed by the smart phone having a pressure sensor comprising a touch-screen display. 45. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the measuring the optical signal is performed by a smart watch having an optical source/sensor comprising a controllable digital camera with a controllable flash and the measuring the applied force is performed by the smart watch having a pressure sensor comprising a touch-screen display. 46. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the measuring the optical signal is performed by a computer-based device having an optical source/sensor and the measuring the applied force is performed by the computer-based device having a pressure sensor. 47. The method of claim 45, wherein the pressure sensor comprises a touch-screen display. 48. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the measuring the applied force comprises measuring AC pressure oscillations exerted by the first finger of a user based on heartbeats of the user and the applied pressure signal is also indicative of the AC pressure oscillations. 49. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, wherein the measuring the applied force comprises performing a bandpass filter on the applied pressure signal to provide an AC pressure oscillation signal at frequencies near a heart rate of the user, and determining the peak- to-peak pressure for the AC pressure oscillations over the applied pressure range. 50. The method of any of foregoing claims after claim 29, determining the blood pressure of the user comprises calculating a mean blood pressure (MBP) by determining a maximum peak- to-peak pressure over the applied pressure range, and calculating the DBP by determining an initial minimum peak-to-peak pressure before the MBP occurs and a corresponding applied pressure, and calculating SBP by the equation: SBP = 2xMBP - DBP.
